stories.title: Micron CEO Sanjay Mehrotra Announces $250 Billion Investment for Expanded Artificial Intelligence (AI) Memory Chip Development
stories.simple_summary: A big computer chip maker (Micron) is spending $250 billion to make more special chips used for AI.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and tech companies — you won't see direct changes to your daily life soon.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI, alleging the AI company stole trade secrets
stories.simple_summary: Apple is suing OpenAI (the company behind ChatGPT), saying OpenAI stole Apple's secret technology.
stories.why_it_matters: This is a legal battle between big tech companies; it likely won't affect you unless it slows down AI tools you use.

stories.title: Silicon Valley's next generation feels the AI blues
stories.simple_summary: Young tech workers in Silicon Valley are worried about AI taking their jobs.
stories.why_it_matters: This shows some people in tech are concerned about AI's impact on employment, though it's mainly a story about younger workers' feelings.

stories.title: Meta removes controversial AI feature on Instagram after backlash
stories.simple_summary: Meta (Facebook's parent company) turned off an Instagram feature that created AI fake pictures of people using their public photos without asking permission.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Instagram, this means Meta listened to complaints and removed a feature that could have copied your public photos without your say-so.

stories.title: Meta turns off the Instagram feature that let users make AI deepfakes of public accounts
stories.simple_summary: Meta removed an Instagram tool that let people make fake AI images of other people's accounts without the account owner's permission.
stories.why_it_matters: If you have a public Instagram account, your photos are safer now because Meta won't automatically let others use them to create deepfakes.

stories.title: Apple files lawsuit, accuses OpenAI of stealing trade secrets
stories.simple_summary: Apple is suing OpenAI for allegedly stealing Apple's secret technology.
stories.why_it_matters: This is the same lawsuit as headlines 1, 6, 7, and 8 — a big legal case between tech companies that likely won't affect you directly.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI, alleging artificial intelligence company stole trade secrets
stories.simple_summary: Apple is suing OpenAI, claiming the AI company stole Apple's trade secrets.
stories.why_it_matters: This is the same lawsuit mentioned in other headlines — a legal dispute between big companies, nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI for allegedly stealing hardware secrets
stories.simple_summary: Apple is suing OpenAI and another company, saying they stole Apple's hardware secrets to help build AI products.
stories.why_it_matters: This is ongoing legal drama between tech giants; unless you own an AI company or work in tech, this won't directly affect you.

stories.title: Apple sues OpenAI over alleged trade secret theft
stories.simple_summary: Apple sued OpenAI, claiming the company's leaders directed employees to steal Apple's secrets.
stories.why_it_matters: This is part of the same lawsuit against OpenAI — legal news for tech companies, not something ordinary people need to worry about.

stories.title: Open source AI matters more than ever, according to Hugging Face’s Clem Delangue
stories.simple_summary: Hugging Face (a company that lets people share AI tools freely) is growing fast and says more companies are using open-source AI instead of renting it from big companies.
stories.why_it_matters: This means AI tools are becoming more available to smaller businesses instead of just big tech companies — potentially more options at lower costs.

stories.title: SK Hynix raises $26.5B in the biggest foreign IPO in US history, is urged to build new US fabs
stories.simple_summary: A Korean chip company raised $26.5 billion (the largest by a foreign company in US history) and is being asked to build factories in America.
stories.why_it_matters: This is mainly investor news, though building US factories could mean more jobs — still mostly relevant to investors and business analysts.

stories.title: Hugging Face’s CEO on why companies are done renting their AI
stories.simple_summary: The CEO of Hugging Face says companies are moving away from renting AI tools from big companies and instead using free, shared AI tools they can own and control.
stories.why_it_matters: This suggests smaller businesses may soon have easier and cheaper access to AI tools instead of paying big companies like OpenAI for service.
